commands.repair_reason: Human review should hold merge until contributor proof exercises the actual patched OpenClaw code path; there is no concrete ClawSweeper repair to apply.; Cleared: The diff reduces a regex-injection footgun and does not add dependencies, workflow changes, package changes, secret handling, or new code-execution surface. (sha=7750bf059213fbf3880368884b04997380b1dbf0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR imports the existing regex escaping helper, wraps the oversized transcript field-name regex interpolation with it, and adds an oversized transcript metadata regression test.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +33. Total +34 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Partially: source inspection and the PR's standalone proof show that metacharacter field names would be interpreted as regex syntax. This is not a high-confidence production-user reproduction because current OpenClaw callers pass hardcoded safe field names.
commands.review_followup: Human review should hold merge until contributor proof exercises the actual patched OpenClaw code path; there is no concrete ClawSweeper repair to apply.

target.title: fix(security): escape field names in transcript regex extraction
target.body:
## Summary

`extractJsonStringFieldPrefix` and `extractJsonNullableStringFieldPrefix` in `session-utils.fs.ts` interpolate the `field` parameter directly into `new RegExp(...)` without escaping. All current callers pass hardcoded field names (`"id"`, `"parentId"`, `"type"`, `"role"`), but the function signature accepts any `string`. A future caller passing a field containing regex metacharacters (e.g. `"foo.bar"`) would match unintended patterns (`.` matches any character instead of a literal dot).

This wraps the interpolation with `escapeRegExp()` from `src/shared/regexp.ts` so metacharacters are treated literally.

## Verification

219 session-utils tests pass (gateway-core, gateway-server, gateway-client). The new `oversized line metadata extraction preserves id and parentId` test exercises the escapeRegExp-guarded regex path.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Regex injection via unescaped field name interpolation in oversized transcript metadata extraction (`session-utils.fs.ts:276,293`). Metacharacter field names cause extraction failure or wrong-value extraction.

**Real environment tested:** macOS arm64, Node 26.0.0, openclaw checkout at patched head.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Standalone runtime script that replicates the vulnerable and fixed extraction functions, then runs each against JSON prefixes containing field names with regex metacharacters:

```
node --import tsx /tmp/proof-85934.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== PR #85934: field name regex escape proof ===

Field with parentheses: count(items)
  Input: {"count(items)":"5","other":"x"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  5

Field with brackets: tags[0]
  Input: {"tags[0]":"first","tags0x":"other"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  first

Field with plus: rate+tax
  Input: {"rate+tax":"15","ratetax":"10"}
  Before fix: 10
  After fix:  15

Field with $: $ref
  Input: {"$ref":"#/definitions/model","ref":"fallback"}
  Before fix: EXTRACTION FAILED
  After fix:  #/definitions/model

Safe field name (normal transcript): id
  Input: {"type":"message","id":"msg-abc123","parentId":"root"}
  Before fix: msg-abc123
  After fix:  msg-abc123
  Both work correctly for safe field names.

escapeRegExp neutralizes metacharacters while safe names are unaffected.
```

Without escapeRegExp, parentheses, brackets, and dollar signs cause extraction to fail entirely, and the plus sign causes extraction of the wrong value (`10` from `"ratetax"` instead of `15` from `"rate+tax"`). With escapeRegExp, all metacharacter field names are treated literally and extract the correct value.

**Observed result after fix:** The escapeRegExp wrapper fixes four classes of metacharacter failures (parentheses, brackets, plus, dollar sign) while leaving safe field names unaffected. The plus-sign case is the most dangerous: it silently returns the wrong value instead of failing.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end production transcript pipeline with adversarial field names (all current callers use hardcoded safe strings). The fix is defense-in-depth.
target.author: sebtardif

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
